title: Barracks conditions at 5 largest Army bases

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act being made by The War Horse, a nonprofit national news organization that covers the military and veterans’ issues.
Pursuant to the federal Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I request records concerning barracks and unaccompanied enlisted housing conditions at the following U.S. Army installations:

Fort Bragg, North Carolina;
Fort Hood, Texas;
Fort Campbell, Kentucky/Tennessee;
Fort Benning, Georgia;
Joint Base Lewis-McChord, Washington.

For purposes of this request, “barracks” and “unaccompanied enlisted housing” include barracks, bachelor enlisted quarters, dormitories, and other government-owned housing primarily used for unaccompanied enlisted soldiers.

The time period for this request is Oct. 1, 2021, through the date this request is processed.

I request the following records:

Records sufficient to show formal inspections, assessments, audits, reviews, or command inspections of barracks or unaccompanied enlisted housing at each listed installation.
Records sufficient to show deficiencies, violations, corrective-action plans, remediation plans, repair priorities, or habitability concerns identified during those inspections or assessments.
Records sufficient to show the number of barracks rooms, buildings, or bed spaces at each listed installation that were designated during the requested period as unavailable, uninhabitable, condemned, closed, restricted, under major repair, or otherwise unsuitable for occupancy.
Records sufficient to show maintenance work-order summaries, service-request summaries, repair-ticket summaries, or facilities complaint summaries for barracks or unaccompanied enlisted housing at each listed installation. Please include, where available, the date opened, date closed, building number, issue category, priority level, and final disposition. I am not seeking the names, phone numbers, personal email addresses, or other personally identifying information of individual soldiers.
Records sufficient to show known or recurring issues involving mold, water intrusion, sewage, pests, heating, ventilation, air conditioning, plumbing, electrical systems, fire safety, broken locks, overcrowding, or other unsafe or unsanitary barracks conditions.
Records sufficient to show briefings, memoranda, reports, slide decks, or written updates provided to installation, garrison, or senior Army leadership concerning serious or recurring barracks deficiencies at any of the listed installations.
Records sufficient to show deferred maintenance estimates, unfunded requirements, sustainment/restoration/modernization needs, military construction requests, or other funding shortfalls related to barracks or unaccompanied enlisted housing at each listed installation.

For any spreadsheets, databases, trackers, inspection logs, work-order exports, or structured data, please provide the records in their native electronic format, including Excel, CSV, or another machine-readable format where available.

Please include attachments to responsive emails, memoranda, reports, presentations, or briefings.

To reduce burden, I am not requesting individual soldiers’ names, Social Security numbers, personal contact information, medical records, or disciplinary records. I am also not requesting every email mentioning barracks. This request seeks records sufficient to show the condition of barracks, known deficiencies, maintenance trends, habitability concerns, leadership awareness, and repair or funding needs at the listed installations.

As a representative of The War Horse, a full-time newsroom dedicated to covering military and veterans’ issues, I ask that any and all fees and other charges be waived as this request is in the public interest. This request is not intended for commercial use.  In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request.

I ask that this request receive expedited processing under 28 CFR 16.5(d)(1)(ii), as The War Horse’s primary purpose is to “inform the public about an actual or alleged Federal Government activity” and 28 CFR 16.5(d)(1)(iv), “[a] matter of widespread and exceptional media interest in which there exist possible questions about the government’s integrity which affect public confidence.” The information gleaned from these documents concerns recent developments that are in the public interest, specifically: the House Armed Services Committee proposal of a bill to award Sgt. Rafael Peralta the Medal of Honor, on May 26, 2026. (See here: https://www.stripes.com/theaters/us/2026-05-27/house-defense-bill-medal-of-honor-rafael-peralta-21798768.html)

If my request is denied in whole or part, I ask that you justify all deletions by reference to specific exemptions of the act. I will also expect you to release all segregable portions of otherwise exempt material. I prefer electronic files and correspondence when available.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Paul Szoldra
Managing Editor, The War Horse News

title: Bricks and Minifigs and Ben Schneiderand

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

I would like to be provided all documents, video, audio, communications,  texts, and emails relating to Brick and Minifigs that have originated since  Feb 1st, 2026

I would like to be provided all documents, video, audio, communications, texts body cam footage, and emails relating to Ben Schneiderand and those arrested at times of his arrest that have originated since Feb 1st, 2026

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

far1
